form_analysis.form_header_text: FY 2027 - Program and Language Appropriations Requests -  SUBMISSIONS DUE 6:00 PM CST 03/09/2026
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
THE HOUSE COMMITTEE ON APPROPRIATIONS HAS RELEASED GUIDANCE FOR FY 2027 APPROPRIATIONS REQUESTS. GUIDANCE CAN BE FOUND: HERE

Please use this form ONLY for programmatic and language funding requests. For community project funding requests, please use our other form on our website.

If you have any questions, please call (202) 225-8885 or email legislation.crockett@mail.house.gov.

fields.label: Please select the type of request:
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this is a programmatic funding request.

fields.label: If you chose "Program Request", please select one of the options below:
options: Support total funding level of: $ (fill in below)
Support last Fiscal Year's (FY23) enacted level
Support the President's FY 2024 budget request
Funding should generally be increased (Choose this only if you DON'T have a specific dollar amount you want to increase the program by)
Funding should generally be reduced (Choose this only if you DON'T have a specific dollar amount you want to reduce the program by)
fields.draft_value: Support total funding level of: $ (fill in below)
fields.rationale: A specific dollar amount ($9,000,000,000) is provided in the funding ask.

fields.label: If you chose "Support total funding level of:", please provide the final funding level at which you wish to see the program funded (i.e. The program was funded at $20,000,000 in last year's Appropriations Bill and you want to increase it by $10,000,000 this year. Therefore, your answer should be $30,000,000):
fields.rationale: Directly uses the specific funding ask amount of $9 billion provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Please write out the language you wish to have inserted into the report:
fields.rationale: The provided context specifies 'N/A' for report language because this is a programmatic funding request for an existing agency account.

fields.label: Please write out the language you wish to have inserted into the bill:
fields.rationale: The provided context specifies 'N/A' for bill language because this is a programmatic funding request for an existing agency account.

fields.label: Provide a very short summary (<500 words) of your request. This summary will be forwarded to the Committee on Appropriations. Please use a word-count program to ensure your summary is not longer than 500 words. You will have an opportunity in the next section to provide a longer justification and explanation of your submission.
fields.draft_value:
* Request for $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to support Earth science, planetary science, astrophysics, and heliophysics.
* Restores funding to levels necessary for mission stability and the advancement of high-priority Decadal Survey goals.
* Ensures the United States maintains its competitive edge in space exploration and scientific discovery against rising international programs.
* Supports high-tech innovation and workforce development through direct investments in academic and industrial research.
fields.rationale: This summary concisely states the $9 billion request and the strategic rationale based on the provided advocacy talking points and funding ask.

fields.label: Provide a justification for your project. Why is this project important to TX-30?  What does it seek to accomplish? What is the benefit to the taxpayer? This answer will be used by Congresswoman Crockett's office to rank the project.
fields.draft_value:
* Supports local scientific leadership at Southern Methodist University, which has received over $648,000 in recent NASA Science grants for critical research.
* Funds active SMU projects investigating ancient Moon magma systems, directly contributing to the scientific foundations of the Artemis program.
* Generates high taxpayer value by providing the data required for precise weather forecasting, disaster response, and planetary defense.
* Every dollar invested in NASA Science returns roughly $3 to the American economy, driving growth in the North Texas technology sector.
fields.rationale: This justification links the national request to the $648,501 in grant funding at Southern Methodist University (SMU) and the specific lunar research project mentioned in the NASA spending context.

fields.label: How would you rank this request compared to any other requests you submitted?
fields.rationale: Priority ranking requires internal knowledge of all submissions from the constituent.

fields.label: List any other Members of Congress to whom you are submitting this same request:
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Provide the name, phone number, and email of the submitting organization (DO NOT list information for a government affairs firm):
Provide First and Last name of the person best able to answer questions on this request. (either someone at the recipient organization or a government affairs firm.)
Provide the physical address of the enttity for whom this request is submitted on behalf of (DO NOT list the address of the government affairs firm)

If this request was filled out by a person from a government affairs firm, please list the name of the individual filling out the form, the name of the firm, and contact information (this can be repeated from the prior question)
Please list what jobs, if any, in the district or state are created, supported, or impacted by this program/request.
fields.draft_value:
* Directly supports faculty researchers and graduate students at Southern Methodist University through four grants in FY2023-FY2025 (1 currently active).
* Bolsters the aerospace and technology talent pipeline in the Dallas-Fort Worth region by funding advanced STEM research opportunities.
* Impacts thousands of regional contractor and civil servant positions that rely on stable, predictable NASA funding cycles.
fields.rationale: The response focuses on SMU researchers and the broader regional impact, consistent with the local spending data and general advocacy talking points.

fields.label: Please describe what instrastruycture and environmental impact this program/request has on TX-30
fields.draft_value:
* Utilizes academic and laboratory infrastructure at Southern Methodist University to conduct mineral analysis and planetary research.
* NASA Earth Science missions provide the essential satellite data used by local infrastructure managers for climate resilience and flood monitoring.
* Programmatic funding has a negligible environmental footprint while producing data that informs environmental protection efforts across Texas.
fields.rationale: This identifies SMU's role as a local research hub and connects NASA's Earth Science mission to regional environmental data needs.

fields.label: Please include a quote from your organization (that can be attributed) on the importance of the program:
fields.draft_value: * "Investing in NASA Science is an investment in American leadership; it secures our position as the world's premier spacefaring nation while inspiring the next generation of Dallas-based innovators."
fields.rationale: A high-level strategic quote aligned with the requested persona and advocacy tone.

fields.label: Press Contact - Name/Email/Phone
